itemListElement.reviewBody:
We have been associated with Tatva school for over three years now. Like many parents, we were initially concerned after reading the older reviews before enrolling our child. However, our perspective completely changed the moment we visited the campus and experienced the environment firsthand.

Having had our son study in three different schools prior to this, we feel confident in saying that this institution truly stands apart in its approach. Rather than focusing solely on rote learning, the school emphasizes practical understanding and conceptual clarity. The teachers are not only knowledgeable but also approachable and patient, always willing to address students’ doubts with care.

What we appreciate most is the school’s commitment to shaping well-rounded individuals, not just academically driven students. The focus extends beyond textbooks to nurturing good values and behavior. Even the parent-teacher meetings are meaningful, often centered around understanding the child’s development and behavioral growth, rather than just academic performance.

We would genuinely encourage other parents to visit the school and experience it themselves—you may find, as we did, that it offers something truly special.

Hello, We are parents of tatva for 9 years since 2017 with two of our children here. We walked in here without having any understanding of Waldorf or Steiner ( still we don't have full understanding of it ). The only intention  that we had was to provide our children with an alternate education than what we had in our life, so they are able to choose their destiny without getting into the rat race. 

We are still unsure of what made us choose TATVA for our children , may be there was a universe calling or an instinct or a trust. We did go through lot of fears, anxiety, confusion walking this path (especially when we are on a path that's less travelled) and there were several instances when we ourselves / our friends /our parents / our relatives questioned us, if this is the right choice for the children and it instilled in us, lot of fear out of the future. 

The way the children were becoming, as a human with a strong willpower, reinforced it to certain extent, that the choice might be right. And academically there was nothing that was inspiring us materialistically uptil grade 3. 

Only when our child was in grade 4, there was something meaningful, we could visualize in his academic part and the same, we could sense in every child who walked this path along with us, were in par with any other children. This gave us the confidence further to continue on the journey. Moreover, looking at all other aspects of the children from a physical, health, emotional, thoughtful perspective, they were exceedingly beyond our imagination. 

We also wondered at how each child is unique and the way the walk the path was unique too. Yet one common thing that we felt, the children were all very authentic and truthful, with a clear sense of purpose. 

Now, after 9 years, we don't regret on our decision at all and there is no looking back too. But has our fear out of the future has gone completely? May be not fully. Still we do have questions around, how would the children be when he passes out of the school and how would their college admissions would be. But this 9 year journey, was not only for the children. We could deeply feel that, the journey has shaped our lives as a family too. We do realize that we have become a better social human being, our will has strengthened a lot and our thoughts have become free. While we look out of the future 10,15 years from now, we equally learnt to look at it incrementally and what is in our control and be fully present to it. That soothens many things and actually things are blossoming beautifully, much more than what we anticipated. 

Looking back on the path travelled so far, it was not a smooth journey all the time. We had twists and turns, ups and downs and it was smooth as well as rough. We enjoyed as well as debated with the school. We did have fee hikes which were challenging to manage, we did have challenges with the school operations, staff,  management , we did face challenges with our own children struggling to cope up. But every time there were souls at school listening to us at heart and cleared the path and we were able to move forward and each time the bond with the school, parents strengthened.

Finally, now we are clear, that the school has laid down (infact, laying down ) the path and it's us, who have choosen to walk the path for the children along with them. It is very much essential for us hold our hands together with children, fellow parents , teachers and the school , because we could sense the beautiful life just at the reach of the children and we need to walk a few more yards with love, courage and wisdom. Thank you.
School for prince and princess in a fairy tale world. You will have to take care of your ward lifelong. No waldrof just name sake, Arrogant management, more arrogant teachers. Total waste of money.

not recommeding one bit. 
After payment of fees said "take your ward and leave" in the middle of the academic year.
sweet until admission when complained about coordinator principal madam said same thing. arrogant peoples. 
all schools wash off responsibilities but they are cheating different level using cambridge 
read tatva confessions
Thank God, we pulled out at the last minute and enrolled in another school nearby. Do due diligence and don't be misled by Waldorf claimns. everything PR stunt nowadays

Lack of communication between class teachers and parent . 

Communication book will be provided to kid,but something important will not be write down there , instead parent rep send in wats app where there may chance of miss the info if we get stuck with other family and office issues .

All burden over parents .My kid teacher  don't stand on one point she change her comments according to situation. 
Teachers not trained to take their job with service mentality they were very rude to parents , and felt stress to handle only 20 kids.

For everything they blame it's due to media or parent not making kids to sleep or diet is not good. They don't think what can be changed from their end .
Wonderful school.From Van driver,Akkas, teacher's to admin very much caring..Kids will enjoy and study..They will learn lot of skills.

1. Lack of communication: We were asked to reach the school premise along with our child, and there was a confusion of which campus to go. We reached the primary school and then we were redirected to the main campus

2. Lack of empathy: The POC for admission, Sabareeswari was busy with other parents. My kid sat in her chair and was playfully insistent. I thought Sabareeswari would be ok to sit in the adjacent sofa, but however she wanted to sit in the very same chair, which left my kid sobbing uncontrollably. I was utterly perplexed as I expected the team from a Waldorf based school, to have a certain level of empathy to understand the kid.

PS: Post 10 to 15 minutes,  I requested Sabareeswari to kindly sit in the sofa, for which she agreed, but still, I would have expected much more empathy from a Waldorf inspired school facilitator.
